reasoning: By interpreting "as high or higher ranking" than 15 as ranking 1st to 15th (i.e. Rank $\le 15$), the likelihood of Hans Niemann maintaining or improving his ranking heavily depends on his performance in the ongoing U.S. Chess Championship (Oct 2025). As a 2730+ player facing a deeply competitive field where many players are 2600-2690, drawing games leads to a rating penalty. Because he is currently sitting at or near his career peak ranking of #15, regression downwards is more likely than stabilizing or moving up, as a loss of just a few rating points would cause a drop to the #16-20 range given the dense packing of players between 2730 and 2750. The official November 2025 FIDE list will be published around November 1, dictating the ranking for the first two resolution dates, resulting in highly comparable probabilities for those two dates. The third date captures January 2026, where the forecast horizon is extended, adding uncertainty to the long-term trend, but still anchored below 50% given the difficulty of maintaining a top 15 spot among elite super-GMs over a multi-month period without significant classical tournament successes.
